title: A Distributed Gaussian Process Model for Multi-Robot Mapping
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: Presents DistGP, a multi-robot algorithm for fitting an arbitrary global function using only local observation and computation with peer-to-peer communication. Uses sparse GPs with inducing points that act as local summaries and enables decentralized inference with GBP.

methodology.innovation: Loopy model that relaxes tree constraint to allow cycles

key_contributions: A new distributed GP model trainable with GBP that outperforms TSGPs
Empirical analysis showing the model can be built on-the-fly with distributed, asynchronous inference
Experimental evaluation showing superior performance to DiNNO
related_work: DiNNO (Distributed NN optimiser) by Yu et al.
Other distributed neural maps using CADMM or direct regularization

technical_details.inducing_points: Gaussian distributed, act as local summaries
technical_details.consistency_constraints: Local rather than global weight-space constraints
technical_details.scalability: Each robot stores only a local map estimate
